summary: Updated bye skill to use `tmux kill-session` instead of sending /exit keystrokes to the terminal.
detail: Decisions: Replaced tmux send-keys /exit + kill-pane sequence with a direct tmux kill-session call — eliminates the security prompt that required user acknowledgment on every /bye invocation

summary: Replaced tmux send-keys /exit + kill-pane sequence with a direct tmux kill-session call
detail: eliminates the security prompt that required user acknowledgment on every /bye invocation

summary: Added a SessionStart hook to ~/.claude/settings.json that runs setup-dev-panes.sh at session start, and configured the dev-panes tmux layout for the current session.
detail: Decisions: Added dev-panes setup-dev-panes.sh as an async SessionStart hook rather than a blocking one — avoids delaying session startup if tmux is unavailable or pane already exists; Used $PWD as the argument so the script receives the working directory at session start, matching the manual invocation pattern

summary: Added dev-panes setup-dev-panes.sh
detail: an async SessionStart hook rather than a blocking one

summary: Used $PWD
detail: the argument so the script receives the working directory at session start, matching the manual invocation pattern

Ran skill-review on skills/search-sessions. Produced a review document at /private/tmp/claude/skill-review-search-sessions.md with 6 issues. No edits applied yet — awaiting user confirmation.

summary: Added tmux pane state retention to compaction-instructions skill so post-compaction sessions know what display panes are open and what they contain.
detail: Decisions: Added paragraph to compaction-instructions/SKILL.md instructing it to enumerate active tmux panes (ID, content, workflow type) in the /compact message — chosen because pane state is ephemeral context that compaction was silently dropping, causing post-compact sessions to incorrectly assume no panes were open

summary: Added paragraph to compaction-instructions/SKILL.md instructing it to enumerate active tmux panes (ID, content, workflow type) in the /compact message
detail: chosen

detail: Decisions: Added new section 'Referencing Shared Plugin Resources' to skill-construction-guidance.md rather than SKILL.md — it belongs in the construction guidance because that's the authoritative reference for skill authoring patterns, and SKILL.md delegates there for Workflow 2.; Placed the new section immediately after the intro block and before 'Writing Effective Descriptions' — shared resource patterns are structural/architectural knowledge that should be available before any other authoring guidance is read.

summary: Rewrote notify-me skill Workflow 2 to use launchd instead of `at`/`sleep`. Added Workflow 3 (list pending notifications) and Workflow 4 (cancel a notification). Bumped plugin version 1.0.29 → 1.0.30.
detail: Decisions: Chose launchd StartCalendarInterval over `at` — no sudo required, survives reboots, fires on next wake after sleep, available on all modern macOS without enabling atd.; Named launchd jobs local.claude-notify.YYYYMMDDHHmm (fire time, zero-padded) — self-describing, sortable, and consistent across Claude sessions so any instance can list or cancel jobs created by another.; Stored helper scripts in ~/.claude-notify/ alongside plists in ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ — keeps Claude-created artifacts discoverable and separable from user-created agents.; Scripts self-clean on fire (unload plist + rm both files) to avoid stale LaunchAgents accumulating over time.; Dropped the sleep fallback entirely — launchd is universally available on macOS and the fallback added complexity without meaningful benefit.

summary: Added 2-minute buffer rule to Workflow 2 Step 1 for relative time requests under 3 minutes. Bumped version to 1.0.31.
detail: Decisions: Added 2-minute buffer when requested fire time is less than 3 minutes away — launchd setup takes ~30s and a tight window risks missing the scheduled minute.; Kept bash wrapper for self-cleanup despite macOS background activity prompt — user accepted the tradeoff; osascript-direct approach rejected because it skips plist cleanup.

summary: Added relative day expression handling to Workflow 2 Step 1 (in N days, next Thursday, etc.). Version held at 1.0.31.
Dropped bash wrapper from notification plists (osascript direct). Added Workflow 5 watchdog (weekly cleanup). Updated Workflows 3/4 for no companion script file.
detail: Decisions: Dropped bash wrapper from notification plists — call osascript directly to eliminate per-notification background activity prompts.; Moved cleanup to a weekly watchdog (Workflow 5) — bash registers once at login, prompting only on first install.; Watchdog fires every Sunday at 3AM with RunAtLoad:true — cleans stale plists on first install immediately.; Message embedded directly in plist ProgramArguments — Workflow 3 extracts from plist via grep rather than companion .sh file.

summary: Re-enabled calendar in radar skill. Added meeting-notifiers.md: post calendar in chat, user claims meetings, schedule 5-min notifications, build show-and-go with claimed/team/personal only.
detail: Decisions: Calendar re-enabled despite RSVP visibility gap — user accepts imperfect status values; the meeting-notifiers claim step compensates by letting user declare attendance explicitly.; Show-and-go built after user claims meetings, not waited on — Radar posts calendar in chat, schedules notifiers, then opens pane without waiting for it to close.; Unclaimed new/maybe events treated as declined and omitted from show-and-go — keeps agenda focused on committed meetings only.; Notification message format: Starting in 5: {name}, truncated to 60 chars total with ... if needed.

summary: Added CLAUDE.md bootstrap check to compaction-instructions skill so it self-installs the Auto-Compact Instructions section on any machine missing it.
detail: Decisions: Added CLAUDE.md check as a step within the skill rather than a separate skill or hook — keeps the bootstrap logic co-located with the compaction workflow that depends on it; Check uses section header presence (grep for '# Auto-Compact Instructions') not full content match — avoids false negatives from minor wording drift while still catching missing sections; If section exists, prompt user before updating rather than auto-overwriting — user may have intentional customizations in their CLAUDE.md
